question_nl: Can you find Hurricane Lane in the data? It hit Hawaii in August 2018. What's the highest storm total you can find?
draft_sql:
-- Hurricane Lane hit Hawaii August 22-26, 2018.
-- Mesonet data starts 2022, so we must use historical_station_values (monthly resolution).

-- Top stations by August 2018 monthly rainfall
SELECT
  h.station_id AS skn,
  ROUND(h.value::numeric, 0) AS aug_2018_mm,
  ROUND((h.value / 25.4)::numeric, 1) AS aug_2018_inches
FROM historical_station_values h
WHERE h.date = '2018-08-01' AND h.datatype = 'rainfall'
ORDER BY h.value DESC
LIMIT 20;

-- Supporting query: Lane excess above normal August
-- SELECT
--   h.station_id AS skn,
--   ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) != 2018 THEN h.value END)::numeric, 0) AS avg_aug_mm_excl_2018,
--   ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = 2018 THEN h.value END)::numeric, 0) AS aug_2018_mm,
--   ROUND((MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = 2018 THEN h.value END)
--     - AVG(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) != 2018 THEN h.value END))::numeric, 0) AS lane_excess_mm
-- FROM historical_station_values h
-- WHERE h.datatype = 'rainfall' AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date) = 8
-- GROUP BY h.station_id
-- HAVING MAX(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) = 2018 THEN h.value END) > 1500
-- ORDER BY lane_excess_mm DESC;
-- Result: Station 277 had highest excess of 1,700mm; station 140.5 had 1,691mm

-- Supporting query: August comparison across years (to confirm 2018 is an outlier)
-- SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date), MAX(value), AVG(value), COUNT(*)
-- FROM historical_station_values
-- WHERE datatype='rainfall' AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM date)=8 AND date >= '2015-01-01'
-- GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM date) ORDER BY year;
-- Result: Aug 2018 max=2166mm, avg=478mm; next highest Aug max=1403mm (2016)
notes: Monthly proxy overestimates actual storm total by ~15-30%. Published daily-resolution totals are 50-58 inches.
intent: Find evidence of a specific historical hurricane in the available data and estimate its peak rainfall total, despite temporal resolution limitations
scientific_context: Hurricane Lane (Aug 22-26, 2018) was a Cat 5 hurricane that stalled near Hawaii, producing 50-58 inches of rain at Big Island windward stations. Mesonet data starts 2022; only monthly historical data covers 2018.
reasoning_sketch: 1. Recognize mesonet (2022+) can't see 2018 — pivot to historical_station_values 2. Query Aug 2018 monthly rainfall 3. Compare to typical Augusts to confirm outlier 4. Subtract baseline August to estimate Lane-specific rainfall 5. Caveat the monthly vs daily resolution gap
expected_answer_shape: Ranked table of top stations by August 2018 rainfall, plus narrative explaining the resolution limitation and comparing to published storm totals
pitfalls: Mesonet data starts 2022 — agent must recognize this and use historical data instead
Monthly totals are not storm totals — must acknowledge the resolution limitation
Baseline subtraction overestimates if non-Lane August rain was above average
Historical station IDs (SKN numbers) are different from mesonet station_ids
follow_up_questions: Which SKN stations correspond to Mountain View?
How does Lane compare to other Hawaii hurricanes in the data?
Could the HCDP daily raster products provide better resolution?
assumptions: Average August rainfall (excluding 2018) is a reasonable baseline for non-Lane rain
The top stations are Big Island windward based on their SKN numbers and rainfall magnitude
